name: Lincoln (United States)

colors.symbolism: the field, recalling the Nebraska state flag
the central disc framing the city emblem
the silhouette of the Nebraska State Capitol and the lettering 'CITY OF LINCOLN'
the pioneer figure flanking the central emblem
the corn stalks and prairie plants flanking the central emblem

description: A blue field bearing a red disc at the centre, charged with a white silhouette of the Nebraska State Capitol flanked by yellow pioneer figures and green corn stalks, with the inscription 'CITY OF LINCOLN' encircling the device.
history: Lincoln's first municipal flag, adopted in 1931. The design centred on the Nebraska State Capitol (then nearing completion) and was used until 2022, when the city replaced it with a new, simpler design following a public competition.

features.role: central red disc framing the city emblem
features.symbolism: Frames the central depiction of the Nebraska State Capitol.

features.role: Nebraska State Capitol ('Tower of the Plains')
features.symbolism: Lincoln is the capital of Nebraska; the Bertram Goodhue–designed State Capitol, completed in 1932, is the city's defining landmark.

features.role: pioneer figures flanking the central emblem
features.symbolism: Recall the homesteaders and farmers who settled the Nebraska prairie.

features.role: stalks of corn / prairie plants flanking the emblem
features.symbolism: Nebraska's agricultural economy and the prairie landscape around Lincoln.
